qa_pairs.question: What is the general characteristic of the clusters mentioned, regarding uniformity?
qa_pairs.answer: While the clusters, particularly the general purpose ones, have a relatively high degree of uniformity, there are still significant distinctions to be aware of when moving between them.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended method for transferring data between clusters when the amount is large?
qa_pairs.answer: For transferring data from one cluster to another, especially when the amount of data exceeds a few hundred megabytes, the use of Globus is recommended.

qa_pairs.question: How can users access each cluster, and what about usernames and passwords?
qa_pairs.answer: Each cluster is accessible via SSH by simply changing the name of the cluster. Your username and password are the same across all of the clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any special steps required to access the Niagara cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, accessing Niagara does require a further step.

qa_pairs.question: Do the general purpose clusters have a similar filesystem structure?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, each of the general purpose clusters has a similar filesystem structure.

qa_pairs.question: Is data mirrored between the filesystems of different clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, there is no mirroring of data between the clusters. The contents of your home, scratch, and project spaces are independent on each cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Can filesystem quota policies differ between clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the quota policies may differ between clusters, though generally not by much.

qa_pairs.question: If a research group has a special storage allocation on one cluster, will it be available on other clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if a group has a special storage allocation, for example `$HOME/projects/rrg-jsmith`, it will normally only be available on that particular cluster.

qa_pairs.question: If a group's default project space quota is increased on one cluster, does this change apply to other clusters as well?
qa_pairs.answer: No, if your group requested that the default project space quota on a cluster be increased from 1 to 10 TB, this change will only have been made on that specific cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Are globally installed software modules the same across all general purpose clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the collection of globally installed modules, distributed using CVMFS, is the same across all of our general purpose clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to Python virtual environments, R packages, or Perl packages installed in a user's directories when moving to a new cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Any Python virtual environments or R and Perl packages that you may have installed in your directories on one cluster will need to be re-installed on the new cluster, using the same steps employed on the original cluster.

qa_pairs.question: If a user modified their `$HOME/.bashrc` file on one cluster, what action is needed on a new cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: If you modified your `$HOME/.bashrc` file on one cluster to customize your environment, you will need to modify the same file on the new cluster you're using.

qa_pairs.question: If a program was installed in a user's directories on one cluster, does it need to be re-installed on a new cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you installed a particular program in your directories, this will also need to be re-installed on the new cluster since the filesystems are independent between clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What system do all clusters use for job submission?
qa_pairs.answer: All of our clusters use Slurm for job submission.

qa_pairs.question: What variations should users be aware of regarding CPU cores and memory when submitting jobs across clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should note that the number of CPU cores per node or per GPU, and the amount of memory per node or per core, may vary significantly, so they may need to adapt their scripts.

qa_pairs.question: Do compute nodes on all clusters have direct Internet access?
qa_pairs.answer: No, on some clusters, compute nodes may not have direct Internet access. Users should access the cluster's link to find out about site-specific policies.

qa_pairs.question: Are default compute allocations available on every cluster for research groups?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, each research group has access to a default allocation, such as `#SBATCH --account=def-jsmith`, on every cluster.

qa_pairs.question: Are special compute allocations, like RRG or contributed allocations, available across all clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, special compute allocations like RRG or contributed allocations are tied to a particular cluster and will not be available on other clusters.
